content: Anticoagulation reversal for major/life-threatening bleeding: Warfarin: 4-factor prothrombin complex concentrate (PCC) 25-50 units/kg + IV vitamin K 5-10mg (FFP if PCC unavailable). Dabigatran: idarucizumab 5g IV (monoclonal antibody fragment, immediate reversal); hemodialysis also removes dabigatran. Factor Xa inhibitors (rivaroxaban, apixaban, edoxaban): andexanet alfa (recombinant modified factor Xa decoy) 400-800mg IV bolus + infusion (approved for life-threatening bleeding); 4-factor PCC 50 units/kg if andexanet unavailable (off-label but commonly used). Minor bleeding: hold anticoagulant, local measures, consider dose reduction when resuming. Perioperative management: low bleeding risk procedures—continue DOAC, skip morning dose day of procedure; moderate-high risk—hold DOAC 2-4 days before (longer if renal impairment); warfarin hold 5 days, bridge with LMWH if high thrombotic risk (mechanical mitral valve, recent VTE <3 months, AF with CHA2DS2-VASc ≥5). Resume anticoagulation 24-72 hours post-procedure depending on bleeding risk.
